name: Nginx UI
what_it_does: A self-hosted web UI for managing Nginx servers with real-time monitoring, online configuration editing with validation, SSL certificate automation via Let's Encrypt, multi-server cluster support, and an MCP interface for AI-assisted configuration management.
use_cases: Managing Nginx configurations across multiple servers from a single web interface
Automating SSL certificate provisioning and renewal via Let's Encrypt
Real-time monitoring of CPU, memory, load, and disk usage on Nginx hosts
Version-controlled configuration changes with backup, comparison, and rollback
AI-assisted Nginx configuration optimization via the built-in MCP interface
not_for: Teams using Apache, Caddy, or other web servers
Environments requiring read-only configuration management (UI supports write operations)
Containerized microservice routing where a service mesh or Kubernetes ingress is more appropriate
best_when: When you manage one or more Nginx servers and want a web-based UI with SSL automation, configuration versioning, and optional AI-assisted tuning.
avoid_when: When your infrastructure team prefers pure IaC/GitOps management (Ansible, Terraform) without a web UI layer, or when AGPL licensing is a concern.

auth.notes: Default admin/admin credentials on first setup, customizable. Web terminal access included. No SSO documented in README.

pricing.notes: AGPL-3.0 licensed. Free and open source. Optional sponsorship via GitHub Sponsors and Afdian. Demo at https://demo.nginxui.com (admin/admin).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Configuration changes trigger automatic testing/validation before applying — errors surfaced via UI, MCP error detail unknown.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Configuration apply with validation exists; idempotency of MCP operations not documented.
known_agent_gotchas: Default credentials (admin/admin) must be changed immediately on deployment
AGPL-3.0 license requires open-sourcing modifications if distributing the software
MCP interface documentation is sparse — agent capabilities not fully enumerated in README
Web terminal access expands attack surface if exposed to untrusted networks
Multi-server cluster management requires careful network access configuration between nodes
